claims.claim_text: Hugging Face is a major ecosystem or hub for discovering, understanding, and using open models rather than a single model.
claims.span_hint: lines 35-39, 47 | section: What Hugging Face Actually Is
claims.rationale: The Hub overview describes Hugging Face as a central platform for sharing, exploring, and experimenting with open-source ML models and related resources.

claims.claim_text: A model repo contains model files and configs, and typically includes a model card.
claims.span_hint: line 53 | section: The Three Things Learners Confuse Most
claims.rationale: Hugging Face's model-upload documentation says model repos can include checkpoints and configs and recommends adding a model card to the repo.

claims.claim_text: Tokenizer assets can be stored in a Hugging Face model repo.
claims.span_hint: line 53 | section: The Three Things Learners Confuse Most
claims.rationale: The Transformers tokenizer docs explicitly document pushing tokenizer files to the Model Hub.

claims.claim_text: Transformers is a library used to load and use models, distinct from a model repo.
claims.span_hint: line 57 | section: The Three Things Learners Confuse Most
claims.rationale: The Transformers documentation identifies Transformers as a Hugging Face library for model definition, inference, and training.

claims.claim_text: Model cards cover intended use, limitations, and license information on Hugging Face model pages.
claims.span_hint: lines 69-74 | section: How To Read A Model Page
claims.rationale: The model card documentation says model cards should describe intended uses and limitations, and its metadata supports displaying license information.

claims.claim_text: Community derivatives can be fine-tuned, quantized, or merged versions derived from a base model.
claims.rationale: Hugging Face's model card metadata docs explicitly describe derivative relationships such as fine-tunes, quantized models, adapters, and merges.

further_reading.title: Hugging Face Hub Documentation
further_reading.why_relevant: Gives learners the top-level mental model of the Hub and how models, datasets, Spaces, and repositories fit together.

further_reading.title: Model Cards
further_reading.why_relevant: Shows what information a good model page should contain, including intended use, limitations, and metadata.

further_reading.title: Transformers Documentation
further_reading.why_relevant: Helps learners distinguish the software library from a model repo and understand where loading and inference instructions live.

notes: Most of the module is pedagogical guidance rather than source-dependent factual material; the seed focuses on the ecosystem-description and model-page structure claims that merit inline citations.
